017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.net.http.*;
021import java.net.http.HttpRequest.*;
022import java.net.http.HttpResponse.*;
023
024/**
025 * {@link HttpTransport} implementation backed by the JDK's built-in {@link java.net.http.HttpClient}.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * This is the <b>default</b> transport used by {@link RestClient}.  It is built into the
029 * {@code juneau-rest-client} artifact, requires no external dependencies, and is always available on Java 11+.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * It is auto-discovered via {@link java.util.ServiceLoader} and can also be instantiated explicitly:
033 *
034 * <p class='bjava'>
035 *    <jv>transport</jv> = JavaHttpTransport.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
036 *       .httpClient(HttpClient.newHttpClient())
037 *       .build();
038 *
039 *    <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
040 *       .transport(<jv>transport</jv>)
041 *       .build();
042 * </p>
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
045 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * @since 9.2.1
049 */
050@SuppressWarnings({
051   "resource" // Not owned here; lifecycle is managed by the surrounding context
052})
053public final class JavaHttpTransport implements HttpTransport {
054
055   private final java.net.http.HttpClient httpClient;
056
057   JavaHttpTransport(JavaHttpTransportBuilder builder) {
058      this.httpClient = builder.httpClient != null ? builder.httpClient : java.net.http.HttpClient.newHttpClient();
059   }
060
061   /**
062    * Returns a new builder for this transport.
063    *
064    * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
065    */
066   public static JavaHttpTransportBuilder builder() {
067      return new JavaHttpTransportBuilder();
068   }
069
070   /**
071    * Returns a new instance backed by a default {@link java.net.http.HttpClient}.
072    *
073    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
074    */
075   public static JavaHttpTransport create() {
076      return builder().build();
077   }
078
079   @Override /* HttpTransport */
080   public TransportResponse execute(TransportRequest request) throws TransportException {
081      var jdkRequest = buildJdkRequest(request);
082      HttpResponse<InputStream> jdkResponse;
083      try {
084         jdkResponse = httpClient.send(jdkRequest, BodyHandlers.ofInputStream());
085      } catch (IOException e) {
086         throw new TransportException("HTTP transport error: " + e.getMessage(), e);
087      } catch (InterruptedException e) {
088         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
089         throw new TransportException("HTTP request interrupted", e);
090      }
091      return buildTransportResponse(jdkResponse);
092   }
093
094   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
095   // Internal helpers
096   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
097
098   private static HttpRequest buildJdkRequest(TransportRequest request) {
099      var builder = HttpRequest.newBuilder().uri(request.getUri()).method(request.getMethod(), buildBodyPublisher(request.getBody()));
100      for (var h : request.getHeaders())
101         builder.header(h.name(), h.value());
102      var timeout = request.getTimeout();
103      if (timeout != null)
104         builder.timeout(timeout);
105      return builder.build();
106   }
107
108   @SuppressWarnings({
109      "java:S2095" // PipedInputStream closed by HttpClient when body publishing finishes; PipedOutputStream closed in writer thread try-with-resources
110   })
111   private static BodyPublisher buildBodyPublisher(TransportBody body) {
112      if (body == null)
113         return BodyPublishers.noBody();
114      // Use a pipe so body.writeTo() streams directly to the JDK client without full in-memory buffering.
115      // The writer runs on a daemon thread; the JDK client reads from the PipedInputStream on its own threads.
116      // The supplier runs when the client publishes the request body (not when the HttpRequest is built).
117      return BodyPublishers.ofInputStream(() -> {
118         var in = new PipedInputStream();
119         try {
120            var out = new PipedOutputStream(in);
121            var writer = new Thread(() -> {
122               try (out) {
123                  body.writeTo(out);
124               } catch (IOException e) {
125                  // Closing the pipe on error causes the reader to see an IOException,
126                  // which propagates to the JDK client as a send failure.
127               }
128            }, "juneau-ng-body-writer");
129            writer.setDaemon(true);
130            writer.start();
131            return in;
132         } catch (IOException e) {
133            try {
134               in.close();
135            } catch (IOException e2) {
136               e.addSuppressed(e2);
137            }
138            throw new UncheckedIOException(e);
139         }
140      });
141   }
142
143   private static TransportResponse buildTransportResponse(HttpResponse<InputStream> jdkResponse) {
144      var builder = TransportResponse.builder()
145         .statusCode(jdkResponse.statusCode())
146         .body(jdkResponse.body());
147      jdkResponse.headers().map().forEach((name, values) ->
148         values.forEach(value -> builder.header(name, value)));
149      return builder.build();
150   }
151}
